summary_markdown:
# Issue Summary for #433: Delete `content_type/content/__init__.py`

## What this issue is about
This issue addresses a bug where the `__init__.py` file is truncated when creating a new content type using the `mr.bob` tool. The author points out that deleting this file could resolve the problem.

## What needs to be done
The proposal is to delete the `__init__.py` file located at `bobtemplates/plone/content_type/content/__init__.py` to prevent truncation issues. There may be alternative solutions involving creating the file using a Python hook, but the discussion is still ongoing and lacks a definitive consensus.

## Helpful background
The `__init__.py` file is typically used in Python packages to define aliases for other content types. When `mr.bob` creates a new content type, it can override this `__init__.py`, which disrupts existing code.

## Good first steps
1. Review the existing code and issues on the [repo](https://github.com/plone/bobtemplates.plone).
2. Consider trying the proposed deletion of the `__init__.py` file.
3. If you're interested in contributing further, look into the possibility of implementing the alternative hook-based solution discussed in the comments.
4. Testing the changes and updating documentation may also be necessary before a pull request (PR) is submitted. 

We're excited to have new contributors join this effort! Feel free to reach out for help if needed.
